question: What is “chasing the oil”?
content:
“Chasing the oil” means moving your line deeper and deeper inside as the lane dries out, following the remaining oil so your ball can still hold its line.
When many shots are thrown in the same area, that part of the lane loses oil and becomes a burn zone, causing the ball to hook earlier. To keep the ball from grabbing too soon, you must move:
Feet deeper (left for right-handers)
Target deeper, so that you place the ball in an area with more oil inside
You “chase the oil” to maintain:
Proper skid length
Predictable hook
Miss room
Consistent carry
If you don’t move with the oil, your ball starts reading too early, hooks too much, and misses inside.
